<p>Ludicrous&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</strong></p>

<p>Get One&rsquo;s Ducks in a Row</p>

questions.statement:
<p><span style="font-weight: bolder;">Directions :</span><em>&nbsp;</em>In each of these questions, a sentence is given in which there are two blank spaces. Below each sentence there are four pairs of words denoted by the letters A, B, C and D. Find out which pair of words can be filled up in the blanks to make the sentence meaningfully complete.</p>

<p data-path-to-node="74">The artist&rsquo;s ________ use of chiaroscuro created a sense of ________, drawing the viewer&rsquo;s eye toward the subtle emotional nuances of the subject.</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: bolder;">Directions :</span><em>&nbsp;</em>In each of these questions, a sentence is given in which there are two blank spaces. Below each sentence there are four pairs of words denoted by the letters A, B, C and D. Find out which pair of words can be filled up in the blanks to make the sentence meaningfully complete.</p>

<p data-path-to-node="69">The ________ of the Roman Empire was not a sudden event but a ________ process of internal decay and external pressures.</p>
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is: Option 3 i.e. "dissolution, protracted".

Key Points

  • The word "dissolution" means the closing down or dismissal of an assembly, partnership, or official body; it can also mean disintegration or decay. (विघटन)
    • Example: The dissolution of the partnership was inevitable due to irreconcilable differences.
  • The word "protracted" refers to something that is prolonged or extended in duration. (लंबा खींचा हुआ)
    • Example: The negotiations were protracted, lasting several months before an agreement was reached.
  • The sentence discusses the fall of the Roman Empire, which was a lengthy process involving both internal and external factors. The combination of "dissolution" (referring to the fall) and "protracted" (indicating the long duration) fits perfectly in the context.

Therefore, the correct answer is: Option 3.

Additional Information

Here are the other options explained along with their Hindi meanings and example sentences:

  • Resurgence (पुनरुत्थान): Revival or increase of activity or popularity.
    • Example: The resurgence of interest in classical music has been remarkable in recent years.
  • Desultory (असंबद्ध): Lacking a plan, purpose, or enthusiasm; occurring randomly or sporadically.
    • Example: His desultory efforts at studying did not yield good results.
  • Efflorescence (विकसित होना): The period or state of flowering; a culmination or peak.
    • Example: The cultural efflorescence during the Renaissance brought forth great works of art.
  • Rapid (तेज़): Happening in a short time or at a fast pace.
    • Example: The rapid advancement of technology has transformed our lives.
  • Genesis (उत्पत्ति): The origin or mode of formation of something.
    • Example: The genesis of the idea came during their long discussion on social issues.
  • Convoluted (जटिल): Extremely complex and difficult to follow.
    • Example: The convoluted argument left the audience feeling confused.
